home.title: What is my IP?

home.title: What is my IP? | meuip.me
home.description: Find your public IP address, location, ISP and network details in real time. Fast, accurate and free.

whatIsIp.heading: What is an IP address?
whatIsIp.p1: An IP address (Internet Protocol address) is a unique numerical identifier assigned to every device connected to a network. It works like a postal address for the internet, telling routers where to send and receive data packets.
whatIsIp.p2: There are two types: a private IP used within your local network (like your home Wi-Fi), and a public IP — the address the rest of the internet sees. This tool detects and displays your public IP in real time.

ipv4vsIpv6.heading: IPv4 vs IPv6 — what is the difference?
ipv4vsIpv6.p1: IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses in the familiar "203.0.113.1" format, supporting around 4 billion unique addresses. Because that pool is nearly exhausted, IPv6 was introduced with 128-bit addresses (e.g. "2001:db8::1"), enabling a virtually unlimited number of connected devices.
ipv4vsIpv6.p2: Most modern ISPs provide dual-stack connectivity, meaning you have both an IPv4 and an IPv6 address. Use our IPv6 test tool to check whether your connection fully supports the newer protocol.

whyKnow.heading: Why should you know your IP address?
whyKnow.p1: Knowing your public IP address has many practical uses:
whyKnow.li1: Set up remote access (VPN, SSH, IP cameras) with your source IP
whyKnow.li2: Verify that your VPN is active and actually masking your real IP
whyKnow.li3: Diagnose connectivity issues when contacting your ISP's support
whyKnow.li4: Understand the geographic location websites and services perceive for you

vpn.heading: VPN, privacy and online anonymity
vpn.p1: A VPN (Virtual Private Network) replaces your public IP with the VPN server's IP address, hiding your real location from websites and online services. When connected to a VPN, this site should display the VPN server's IP rather than your home IP.
vpn.p2: However, beware: beyond the IP itself, other vectors can still expose your identity — such as DNS and WebRTC leaks. Use our DNS Leak Test and WebRTC Leak Test tools to make sure your VPN is protecting you end-to-end.
